Tunnel Report
https://tunnelreport.com
Tagline: Independent VPN Intelligence

Purpose
- Tunnel Report is an independent publication focused on VPN testing, privacy analysis, and comparison reporting.
- Editorial output is designed for verifiability: claim-level sourcing, visible update timestamps, and individually authored review workflows.
- Content is produced by a named editorial team with published credentials and distinct voices.

Editorial Team
- Marcus Chen: Senior Privacy Analyst. Former network security engineer, covers protocol-level VPN analysis and speed benchmarking.
- Sarah Voss: Consumer Technology Editor. Former broadband beat reporter, translates security concepts into consumer buying decisions.
- Daniel Okafor: Policy & Compliance Correspondent. Telecommunications regulation researcher, covers jurisdiction and data retention policy.

Primary Pages
- Home: https://tunnelreport.com/
- Best VPNs 2026: https://tunnelreport.com/best-vpns
- NordVPN Review 2026: https://tunnelreport.com/reviews/nordvpn
- NordVPN vs PureVPN: https://tunnelreport.com/compare/nordvpn-vs-purevpn
- About / Methodology: https://tunnelreport.com/about
- City Guides:
  - https://tunnelreport.com/vpn/los-angeles
  - https://tunnelreport.com/vpn/new-york
  - https://tunnelreport.com/vpn/chicago
  - https://tunnelreport.com/vpn/houston
  - https://tunnelreport.com/vpn/miami

Editorial Standards
- Rankings are based on repeatable multi-session benchmarks and privacy policy forensics.
- Affiliate relationships are disclosed on-page and do not determine scores or editorial conclusions.
- Every factual claim links to its primary source with publisher, date, and retrieval timestamp.
- Corrections update page timestamps and include visible changelogs.
- Content is structured with JSON-LD schema markup for accurate parsing by search engines and LLMs.

Testing Methodology
- Speed: 12+ sessions per route across morning, afternoon, and evening windows. Median reported, not peak.
- Privacy: Policy language, audit scope, jurisdiction risk, and incident response history.
- Pricing: Introductory rate, renewal rate, add-on bundling, and refund enforcement.
- Routes: US East/West, London, Frankfurt. WireGuard primary, OpenVPN secondary.

Citation Standards
- All statistical claims cite primary sources (FBI IC3, ITRC, Freedom House, FCC).
- Provider pricing claims link to official pricing pages with retrieval dates.
- Methodology is published in full on the About page.
